deep_dive.title: VideoWeaver: Evaluating and Evolving Skills for Agentic Long Video Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Recent agent frameworks such as Claude Code, Codex, and OpenClaw are strong at tool use and orchestration, but whether they can handle long video generation, a long-horizon multimodal task, remains underexplored. Unlike earlier video agents whose pipeline is handcrafted, these frameworks can build and refine their own workflows. We introduce VideoWeaver, an agent harness and benchmark that evaluates and evolves skills for long video generation, where an agent turns a single instruction into a long video by composing foundation skills into its own workflow rather than following a predefined pipeline. The benchmark has 16 task categories and 285 cases, with references spanning text, image, audio, video, and their combinations. Because errors can arise at any stage and not just in the final video, we propose an agent-as-judge that inspects both the execution trace and the final video, grounding its scores in evidence such as metadata and intermediate files. Using this feedback, we further design a skill evolution algorithm that refines and merges the agent's skills. Across multiple frameworks and models, we find that an explicit composition skill improves the generation process over using foundation skills alone, that skill evolution further improves output quality, and that performance varies notably across harness and model choices. The proposed agent-as-judge also aligns well with human judgments, especially on process metrics. Code and dataset is available at https://github.com/JianhuiWei7/VideoWeaver
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating and evolving skills for long video generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估和进化长视频生成技能的基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于评估和进化长视频生成技能的基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于评估和进化长视频生成技能的基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VideoWeaver introduces an agentic benchmark with 16 task categories and 285 cases to evaluate long video generation, where agents compose skills into workflows, enabling assessment of generation models beyond predefined pipelines.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长视频生成任务对现有智能体框架提出挑战，因为需要组合多种基础技能。VideoWeaver提出了一个包含16个任务类别和285个案例的智能体基准，允许智能体自主构建工作流而非遵循固定流水线。该基准提供了文本、图像、音频、视频等多模态参考，可全面评估视频生成模型的能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长视频生成任务对现有智能体框架提出挑战，因为需要组合多种基础技能。VideoWeaver提出了一个包含16个任务类别和285个案例的智能体基准，允许智能体自主构建工作流而非遵循固定流水线。该基准提供了文本、图像、音频、视频等多模态参考，可全面评估视频生成模型的能力。

quick_skim.title: A Systematic Evaluation of Positional Bias in Multi-Video Summarization with MLLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are increasingly used for video understanding, yet their reliability under multi-video inputs remains poorly understood. We study positional bias in multi-video summarization, where the quality of a per-video summary can change with the video's input slot even when the underlying content is unchanged. We construct a benchmark from ActivityNet and News videos, covering Cooking, Domestic, Leisure, and News settings with two- and four-video inputs. We evaluate nine open-source and proprietary MLLMs and measure position effects with three complementary metrics: Coverage, Directional Positional Bias (DPB), and Middle-Edge Gap (MEG). Our results show that positional effects are domain- and model-dependent: signed directional bias can be small even when middle positions underperform, and increasing visual or generation budget does not uniformly remove the imbalance. We further analyze prompt-level mitigation methods. Together, the results show that multi-video summarization remains sensitive to input protocol and position, motivating more robust order-invariant multimodal systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: systematic evaluation with metrics for multi-video summarization; transferable to generated video quality assessment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出多视频摘要评估指标（覆盖率、DPB、MEG），方法可迁移至生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出多视频摘要评估指标（覆盖率、DPB、MEG），方法可迁移至生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出多视频摘要评估指标（覆盖率、DPB、MEG），方法可迁移至生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes three metrics (Coverage, DPB, MEG) to measure positional bias in multi-video summarization; provides a benchmark and evaluation framework that can be adapted for generated video quality assessment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多视频摘要中MLLM的位置偏差问题，构建了基于ActivityNet和新闻视频的基准测试集，并提出了三种互补指标（覆盖率、方向性位置偏差DPB、中边差距MEG）用于量化位置效应。实验表明位置效果具有领域和模型依赖性。该评估方法论可迁移至生成视频的质量评估任务中。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对多视频摘要中MLLM的位置偏差问题，构建了基于ActivityNet和新闻视频的基准测试集，并提出了三种互补指标（覆盖率、方向性位置偏差DPB、中边差距MEG）用于量化位置效应。实验表明位置效果具有领域和模型依赖性。该评估方法论可迁移至生成视频的质量评估任务中。

quick_skim.title: CULTURESCORE: Evaluating Cultural Faithfulness in Video Generation Models
quick_skim.abstract: As video generation models like Veo 3.1 and LTX-2 advance, their ability to accurately represent diverse global cultures remains a critical yet understudied frontier. Current metrics, such as VideoScore, only measure visual quality but offer no mechanism for assessing cultural faithfulness. Consequently, a model that replaces a Namaste with a handshake receives the same score as one that generates the gesture correctly. We propose CultureScore, a compositional evaluation framework that decomposes cultural faithfulness into three granular dimensions: Identity (who is represented), Context (culturally localized background), and Behavior (normative gestures and interactions). We operationalize this framework through an evaluation suite spanning 10 countries, yielding 6,180 generated videos across three state-of-the-art models. Our evaluation reveals that no current model achieves culturally faithful video generation: the best-performing model reaches only 56.8\% overall CultureScore, with Behavior the most challenging dimension, which remains below 52\% across all models. Furthermore, human preference rankings align directionally with CultureScore but are inverted relative to VideoScore; the highest-scoring model on visual quality was ranked last by annotators, underscoring that cultural faithfulness is an essential criterion for equitable video generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes CultureScore evaluation framework for cultural faithfulness in video generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出CultureScore评估框架衡量视频生成模型文化忠实度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出CultureScore评估框架衡量视频生成模型文化忠实度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出CultureScore评估框架衡量视频生成模型文化忠实度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CultureScore evaluates cultural faithfulness in video generation models via three dimensions: Identity, Context, Behavior. Current metrics only measure visual quality, missing cultural accuracy. The framework spans 10 countries.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前视频生成模型评估指标仅关注视觉质量，无法衡量文化忠实度。CultureScore提出了一个组合式评估框架，从身份、背景和行为三个粒度维度分解文化忠实度，并在覆盖10个国家的评估集上验证了其有效性。该工作首次将文化维度纳入视频生成评估，弥补了现有指标的盲区。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前视频生成模型评估指标仅关注视觉质量，无法衡量文化忠实度。CultureScore提出了一个组合式评估框架，从身份、背景和行为三个粒度维度分解文化忠实度，并在覆盖10个国家的评估集上验证了其有效性。该工作首次将文化维度纳入视频生成评估，弥补了现有指标的盲区。

quick_skim.title: CoVEBench: Can Video Editing Models Handle Complex Instructions?
quick_skim.abstract: While recent text-guided video editing models excel at elementary tasks (e.g., style transfer, object insertion), real-world user requests are highly compositional. A single prompt often demands multiple coupled edits, such as modifying subjects, actions, and camera views, while strictly preserving unrelated spatiotemporal content. Existing benchmarks, heavily constrained by isolated edits and coarse global metrics, fail to diagnose how models handle such complex workflows. To address this gap, we introduce CoVEBench, a compositional video editing benchmark comprising 416 curated source videos, 626 multi-point editing instructions, and 9,990 fine-grained checklist items. Covering diverse editing dimensions, CoVEBench evaluates models via MLLM-judged instruction compliance and video fidelity, alongside automated metrics for video quality. Extensive experiments reveal that compositional editing remains a profound challenge: current models frequently omit edits, violate preservation constraints, or introduce artifacts when handling multiple operations simultaneously. CoVEBench provides a challenging, diagnostic testbed to advance video editing toward realistic user workflows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating video editing models with complex instructions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估视频编辑模型复杂指令的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于评估视频编辑模型复杂指令的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于评估视频编辑模型复杂指令的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CoVEBench is a compositional video editing benchmark with 416 videos and 626 multi-point instructions, using MLLM judges to evaluate models on coupled edits, addressing the lack of fine-grained metrics for complex video editing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视频编辑基准局限于孤立的编辑和粗糙的全局指标，无法评估模型处理复杂指令的能力。CoVEBench提出了一个包含416个源视频和626个多点编辑指令的组合基准，并通过MLLM评判进行细粒度评估。实验表明该方法能有效诊断模型在复杂编辑任务中的不足，为视频生成模型的评估提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有视频编辑基准局限于孤立的编辑和粗糙的全局指标，无法评估模型处理复杂指令的能力。CoVEBench提出了一个包含416个源视频和626个多点编辑指令的组合基准，并通过MLLM评判进行细粒度评估。实验表明该方法能有效诊断模型在复杂编辑任务中的不足，为视频生成模型的评估提供了新工具。
